title: AAAI
text: Home * Organizations * AAAI AAAI Logo [1] AAAI , the Association for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence is a nonprofit scientific society devoted to advancing the scientific understanding of the mechanisms underlying thought and intelligent behavior and their embodiment in machines. AAAI was founded in 1979 under the name American Association for Artificial Intelligence and changed its name in 2007 [2] . Contents 1 Presidents 2 See also 3 Publications and Journals 4 Selected Publications 5 External Links 6 References Presidents [ edit ] Since 1985, Presidents have served a two-year term, preceded by two years as President-Elect, and followed by two years as Past President. Prior to that time, presidents served one year in each position [3] . President term Allen Newell 1979-1980 Edward Feigenbaum 1980-1981 Marvin Minsky 1981-1982 Nils J. Nilsson 1982-1983 John McCarthy 1983-1984 Woodrow W. Bledsoe 1984-1985 Patrick Winston 1985-1987 Raj Reddy 1987-1989 Daniel Bobrow 1989-1991 Patrick Hayes 1991-1993 Barbara Grosz 1993-1995 Randall Davis 1995-1997 David Waltz 1997-1999 Bruce Buchanan 1999-2001 Tom Mitchell 2001-2003 Ron Brachman 2003-2005 Alan Mackworth 2005-2007 Eric Horvitz 2007-2009 Martha E. Pollack 2009-2010 Henry A. Kautz 2010-2012 Manuela M. Veloso 2012-2014 Thomas G. Dietterich 2014-2016 Subbarao Kambhampati 2017 See also [ edit ] Artificial Intelligence AAAI Workshops Publications and Journals [ edit ] AAAI Publications AAAI Digital Library - AI Magazine AAAI Sponsored Journals AAAI Press Selected Publications [ edit ] Edward Feigenbaum ( 2005 ).
